title: EAP Authentication Method Drift and Weak-Method Regression

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 8.3.1 requires strong authentication. Method-drift detection evidences the strength baseline is enforced and visible.
PCI 12.3.3 requires cipher/protocol review at least every 12 months. The daily method-distribution audit is the operational input to that review.
compliance.controlObjective: Operational input to cryptographic-protocol annual review.

compliance.assurance_rationale: IA-2(1) (Network Access to Privileged Accounts) requires MFA / strong auth for privileged access. EAP-method audit evidences enforcement.
compliance.controlObjective: Operational audit of EAP method strength for privileged users.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Daily UC-17.1.39 export, joined with privileged-user lookup.

compliance.assurance_rationale: SC-13 (Cryptographic Protection) requires implementation of approved cryptography. EAP method audit identifies non-approved methods in use.
compliance.controlObjective: Identify and surface non-approved cryptographic auth methods.

compliance.assurance_rationale: A.8.5 (Secure authentication) requires technologies and procedures for authentication. Method-strength visibility evidences operational integrity.
AC.L2-3.5.3 (Multifactor authentication) requires MFA for privileged-account network access. EAP-method audit confirms method strength supports the MFA assertion.
compliance.controlObjective: Operational evidence of MFA-supporting EAP methods.

controlTest.positiveScenario: On a lab endpoint, configure the wireless supplicant to use EAP-MD5. Attempt auth. The auth succeeds at ISE (5400 emitted with EapAuthentication=EAP-MD5). The daily report includes a row with `combined=EAP-MD5` and `is_weak=yes`.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Reconfigure the supplicant to TEAP+EAP-TLS. Auth succeeds with `combined=TEAP+EAP-TLS` and `is_weak=strong`. The daily report does not include this method in the weak section.

dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Authentication* category — every successful auth event records `EapAuthentication` (the inner method) and `EapTunnel` (the outer method). Common values for `EapAuthentication`: `EAP-TLS`, `EAP-FAST`, `EAP-MD5`, `EAP-MSCHAPV2`, `PEAP`, `LEAP`. Common values for `EapTunnel`: `PEAP`, `EAP-FAST`, `TEAP`, `EAP-TTLS`, `none`. Code 5400 (auth success) carries these fields. CIM mapping: `Authentication.method` after a `props.conf` field-alias to map `EapAuthentication`.
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=5400
| rex "EapAuthentication=(?<inner>[^,\s]+)"
| rex "EapTunnel=(?<outer>[^,\s]+)"
| eval combined = if(outer=="none", inner, outer."+".inner)
| stats count by combined
| sort - count
| eval is_weak = case(
    like(combined, "%LEAP%"), "yes",
    like(combined, "%MD5%"), "yes",
    like(combined, "%PEAP+EAP-MSCHAPV2%"), "weak_legacy",
    like(combined, "%TEAP%"), "strong",
    like(combined, "%EAP-TLS%"), "strong",
    true(), "medium")
| where is_weak IN ("yes", "weak_legacy")
description: Detects use of weak or deprecated EAP authentication methods (LEAP, EAP-MD5, PEAP+MSCHAPv2 in environments where TEAP or EAP-TLS is the policy standard). Catches policy drift where deprecated methods are silently re-enabled by a misconfiguration or where new endpoint types are negotiating weaker methods than expected.
value: EAP-MD5 and LEAP are decades-old methods with known cryptographic weaknesses and should not be in active use. PEAP+MSCHAPv2 is acceptable for legacy clients but FedRAMP / DoD / FIPS 140-3 environments require TEAP or EAP-TLS-only. This UC is the daily auditable evidence that the deployment is enforcing the chosen EAP method policy.
implementation: Schedule daily at 06:00 UTC, search window `-1d@d to @d`, trigger on count > 0, no throttle (the daily report is the audit artefact). For real-time regression detection, schedule a second saved search every 30 min comparing the past 30 min's method distribution to a 7d baseline; alert on >2x change in the share of any weak method.
visualization: (1) Pie/bar: count of auths by method last 30d. (2) Table: combined method | count | is_weak. (3) Trend timechart: % of auths by method over 90d (stacked). Reveals regression. (4) Top-N table: users still using weak methods (drives the deprecation campaign).
dataModelAcceleration: Yes — Authentication DM acceleration helps the trend panel run quickly over 90d.

references.title: RFC 9427 — TEAP — Tunnel Extensible Authentication Protocol Version 1
Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services (Splunkbase 1915)

knownFalsePositives: (a) **Legacy device still on-network** — a single old printer doing PEAP+MSCHAPv2 in an EAP-TLS-only environment will appear daily until decommissioned. Maintain `eap_method_exceptions.csv` and exclude. (b) **Network test devices** — penetration-test rigs commonly run LEAP; mark and exclude. (c) **MAB falls back to no EAP** — MAC-Auth-Bypass sessions don't have EAP; the search filters them via the MessageCode (5400 only emits for EAP successes; MAB uses different codes). Verify your search isn't pulling in MAB.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• ISE *Authentication* category at INFO (default).
• `eap_method_exceptions.csv` lookup defining accepted exceptions per device type.
• `device_inventory.csv` for endpoint context.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Validate event flow:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=5400 earliest=-1h
| stats count by EapAuthentication, EapTunnel
```
For a healthy fleet you should see methods you expect. If MD5 or LEAP appears, that's the symptom this UC catches.

Step 2 — Create the search
Daily audit search in `spl` field. Schedule daily at 06:00 UTC.

Real-time regression search (every 30 min):
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=5400 earliest=-30m
| rex "EapAuthentication=(?<inner>[^,\s]+)"
| stats count by inner
| eval current_share = count / sum(count)
| join inner [
    | inputlookup eap_method_baseline.csv
  ]
| eval delta = current_share - baseline_share
| where abs(delta) > 0.10
```
Where `eap_method_baseline.csv` is built nightly from the 7d distribution.

Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as in controlTest.
Field check: every 5400 event must have `EapAuthentication` non-null. If null, TA version too old.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — EAP Method Distribution":
• Pie: 30d distribution.
• Trend timechart: % share by method last 90d.
• Top-N: users with weak methods.
• Top-N: device types with weak methods (cross-ref `device_inventory.csv`).

Deprecation campaign workflow:
• Daily report → ticket per user/device with weak method → owner assignment → reauth on TEAP/EAP-TLS.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **All methods show as 'medium'** — `is_weak` case has gaps; review the eval.
• **Method counts dropped to zero** — *Authentication* category logging severity raised to WARN; lower to INFO.
• **PEAP+MSCHAPv2 share growing month-on-month** — A new device type is being onboarded with PEAP rather than TEAP. Engage the platform team to update the baseline supplicant config.
grandmaExplanation: Devices have many ways to prove who they are when joining the network — some are modern and strong, others are decades old and weak. We list which methods are being used and ring the bell when a weak old method is seen, so we can replace it with a stronger one.
